title: सिद्ध कुञ्जिका स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सिद्ध कुञ्जिका स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीसिद्धकुञ्जिकास्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य सदाशिव ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीत्रिगुणात्मिका देवता।
श्रीदुर्गाप्रीत्यर्थे सप्तशतीपाठांगत्वेन जपे विनियोगः॥

शिव उवाच -

शृणु देवि प्रवक्ष्यामि कुञ्जिकास्तोत्रमुत्तमम्।
येन मन्त्रप्रभावेण चण्डीजापः शुभो भवेत्॥१॥

न कवचं नार्गलास्तोत्रं कीलकं न रहस्यकम्।
न सूक्तं नापि ध्यानं च न न्यासो न च वार्चनम्॥२॥

कुञ्जिकापाठमात्रेण दुर्गापाठफलं लभेत्।
अति गुह्यतरं देवि देवानामपि दुर्लभम्॥३॥

गोपनीयं प्रयत्नेन स्वयोनिरिव पार्वति।
मारणं मोहनं वश्यं स्तम्भनोच्चाटनादिकम्।
पाठमात्रेण संसिद्ध्येत् कुञ्जिकास्तोत्रमुत्तमम्॥४॥

ॐ ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं चामुण्डायै विच्चे।
ॐ ग्लौं हुं क्लीं जूं सः ज्वालय ज्वालय ज्वल ज्वल प्रज्वल प्रज्वल
ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं चामुण्डायै विच्चे ज्वल हं सं लं क्षं फट् स्वाहा॥५॥

नमस्ते रुद्ररूपिण्यै नमस्ते मधुमर्दिनि।
नमः कैटभहारिण्यै नमस्ते महिषार्दिनि॥६॥

नमस्ते शुम्भहन्त्र्यै च निशुम्भासुरघातिनि।
जाग्रतं हि महादेवि जपं सिद्धं कुरुष्व मे॥७॥

ऐंकारी सृष्टिरूपायै ह्रींकारी प्रतिपालिका।
क्लींकारी कामरूपिण्यै बीजरूपे नमोऽस्तु ते॥८॥

चामुण्डा चण्डघाती च यैकारी वरदायिनी।
विच्चे चाभयदा नित्यं नमस्ते मन्त्ररूपिणि॥९॥

धां धीं धूं धूर्जटेः पत्नी वां वीं वूं वागधीश्वरी।
क्रां क्रीं क्रूं कालिका देवि शां शीं शूं मे शुभं कुरु॥१०॥

हुं हुं हुंकाररूपिण्यै जं जं जं जम्भनादिनी।
भ्रां भ्रीं भ्रूं भैरवी भद्रे भवान्यै ते नमो नमः॥११॥

ॐ अं कं चं टं तं पं यं शं वीं दुं ऐं वीं हं क्षं।
धिजाग्रं धिजाग्रं त्रोटय त्रोटय दीप्तं कुरु कुरु स्वाहा॥१२॥

पां पीं पूं पार्वती पूर्णा खां खीं खूं खेचरी तथा।
सां सीं सूं सप्तशती देव्या मन्त्रसिद्धिं कुरुष्व मे॥१३॥

इदं तु कुञ्जिकास्तोत्रं मन्त्रजागर्तिहेतवे।
अभक्ते नैव दातव्यं गोपितं रक्ष पार्वति॥१४॥

यस्तु कुञ्जिकया देवि हीनां सप्तशतीं पठेत्।
न तस्य जायते सिद्धिररण्ये रोदनं यथा॥१५॥

॥ इति श्रीरुद्रयामले गौरीतन्त्रे शिवपार्वतीसंवादे सिद्धकुञ्जिकास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Siddha Kunjika Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Siddha-kunjika-stotra-mantrasya Sadashiva Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Trigun-atmika Devata.
Shri Durga-prityarthe Saptashati-pathangena Jape Viniyogah.

Shiva Uvacha -

Shrinu Devi Pravakshyami Kunjika-stotram Uttamam.
Yena Mantra-prabhavena Chandi-japah Shubho Bhavet.||1||

Na Kavacham Nargala-stotram Kilakam Na Rahasyakam.
Na Suktam Napi Dhyanam Cha Na Nyaso Na Cha Varchanam.||2||

Kunjika-patha-matrena Durga-patha-phalam Labhet.
Ati Guhyataram Devi Devanam Api Durlabham.||3||

Gopaniyam Prayatnena Svayonir Iva Parvati.
Maranam Mohanam Vashyam Stambhano-chchatana-adikam.
Patha-matrena Samsiddhyet Kunjika-stotram Uttamam.||4||

Om Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche.
Om Glaum Hum Klim Jum Sah Jvalaya Jvalaya Jvala Jvala Prajvala Prajvala
Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche Jvala Ham Sam Lam Ksham Phat Svaha.||5||

Namaste Rudra-rupinyai Namaste Madhu-mardini.
Namah Kaitabha-harinyai Namaste Mahisha-ardini.||6||

Namaste Shumbha-hantryai Cha Nishumbhasura-ghatini.
Jagratam Hi Mahadevi Japam Siddham Kurushva Me.||7||

Aimkari Srishti-rupayai Hrimkari Pratipalika.
Klimkari Kama-rupinyai Bija-rupe Namo'stu Te.||8||

Chamunda Chanda-ghati Cha Yaikari Varadayini.
Vichche Chabhayada Nityam Namaste Mantra-rupini.||9||

Dham Dhim Dhum Dhurjateh Patni Vam Vim Vum Vagadhishwari.
Kram Krim Krum Kalika Devi Sham Shim Shum Me Shubham Kuru.||10||

Hum Hum Humkara-rupinyai Jam Jam Jam Jambhana-adini.
Bhram Bhrim Bhrum Bhairavi Bhadre Bhavanyai Te Namo Namah.||11||

Om Am Kam Cham Tam Tam Pam Yam Sham Vim Dum Aim Vim Ham Ksham.
Dhijagram Dhijagram Trotaya Trotaya Diptam Kuru Kuru Svaha.||12||

Pam Pim Pum Parvati Purna Kham Khim Khum Khechari Tatha.
Sam Sim Sum Saptashati Devya Mantra-siddhim Kurushva Me.||13||

Idam Tu Kunjika-stotram Mantra-jagarti-hetave.
Abhakte Naiva Datavyam Gopitam Raksha Parvati.||14||

Yastu Kunjikaya Devi Hinam Saptashatim Pathet.
Na Tasya Jayate Siddhir Aranye Rodanam Yatha.||15||

|| Iti Shri Rudrayamale Gauri-tantre Shiva-Parvati-samvade Siddha-kunjika-stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शिव बोले - हे देवी! सुनो, मैं उत्तम कुञ्जिका स्तोत्र कहता हूँ, जिसके मन्त्र प्रभाव से चण्डी का जप शुभ होता है।

इसके पाठ मात्र से न कवच की आवश्यकता है, न अर्गला स्तोत्र की, न कीलक की, न रहस्य की, न सूक्त की, न ध्यान की, न न्यास की, न अर्चन की। केवल कुञ्जिका पाठ से ही दुर्गा पाठ का सम्पूर्ण फल प्राप्त हो जाता है।

यह अत्यन्त गोपनीय है और देवताओं को भी दुर्लभ है। हे पार्वती! इसे प्रयत्नपूर्वक गोपनीय रखना चाहिए।

ऐंकारी सृष्टि रूपा को, ह्रींकारी प्रतिपालिका को, क्लींकारी कामरूपिणी को, बीजरूपा को नमस्कार है।

जो कुञ्जिका के बिना सप्तशती पढ़ता है, उसे सिद्धि प्राप्त नहीं होती, जैसे वन में रोना व्यर्थ है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीसिद्धकुञ्जिकास्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य सदाशिव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीत्रिगुणात्मिका देवता। ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं बीजम्। चामुण्डा शक्तिः। श्रीदुर्गाप्रीत्यर्थे सप्तशतीपाठाङ्गत्वेन कुञ्जिकास्तोत्रजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Single most powerful key to unlock the full power of Durga Saptashati
Traditionally invoked for removal of the need for separate kavach, argala, kilak recitations
Invokes complete siddhi of all saptashati mantras through one recitation
Provides marana, mohana, vashya, stambhana powers through mere reading
Considered the supreme secret revealed by Lord Shiva to Parvati
Invokes rapid spiritual progress and divine protection
description: Siddha Kunjika Stotram — सिद्ध कुञ्जिका स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Durga. Rooted in Rudrayamala (Gauri Tantra), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to grant rapid spiritual progress and divine protection, single most powerful key to unlock the full power of Durga Saptashati, and grant complete siddhi of all Saptashati mantras through one recitation. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. Comprising 15 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Siddha Kunjika Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Siddha Kunjika Stotram from Rudrayamala in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Most powerful key to Durga Saptashati recitation for complete siddhi.
alsoKnownAs: Hidden Key Stotram of Devi Mahatmya
relatedSearches: Kunjika stotra hidden key Durga Saptashati
Rudra Yamala Tantra Gauri Tantra source
Siddha Kunjika benefits equals Devi Mahatmya
Aim Hreem Kleem Chamundayai Vichche mantra

seoFaqs.question: What does the name 'Siddha Kunjika' mean and why is it called a 'key'?
seoFaqs.answer: Siddha means perfected or accomplished, kunjika means key, and stotram means hymn, so the title literally translates as 'The Perfected Key Hymn'. As Effortless Mind Meditation's commentary and Hindu Mirror's article both explain, the stotram is called a key because it is held to unlock the full fruit of the Durga Saptashati's 700 verses without requiring full recitation of the longer text. Within Devi liturgy, the Saptashati itself is the locked treasure-house of Devi power, and the Siddha Kunjika is the small, specially shaped key that opens it. This is why the hymn is studied as a self-contained mantra-stotra rather than as a verse-by-verse praise; its worth lies in its claimed ability to substitute for the full Saptashati when time or circumstance prevent the longer parayana.

seoFaqs.question: What is the scriptural source of the Siddha Kunjika Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Siddha Kunjika Stotram is traditionally drawn from the Gauri Tantra section of the Rudra Yamala Tantra, framed as a private dialogue between Shiva and Parvati. As Effortless Mind Meditation, Manblunder and Hindu Mirror all record, Shiva discloses the stotram to Parvati after praising the Devi Mahatmya, telling her this is the secret key (atyanta-rahasya) by which the saptashati's full power can be invoked in compressed form. Some popular printings additionally cite the Damara Tantra and the Brahmajnana Tantra as parallel sources for connected verses. The hymn is therefore not part of the Devi Mahatmya proper but a Tantric companion text that the householder tradition has folded into daily and Navratri Devi worship for centuries.

seoFaqs.question: What is the central mantra inside the Siddha Kunjika Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is built around the Navarna Mantra of Devi, 'Aim Hreem Kleem Chamundayai Vichche', expanded with additional bija syllables specific to the Kunjika tradition. As Manblunder's verse-by-verse commentary and Yantrachants' explanation record, the body of the stotram weaves together the bijas of Saraswati (Aim), Mahalakshmi (Hreem) and Mahakali (Kleem) with the central Chamunda invocation, then layers further mantras such as 'Klam, Kleem, Klum, Klaim, Klaum' and 'Hum, Vam, Phat' to address the goddess in her protective and destructive aspects. This high mantric density is one reason traditional teachers recommend the stotram be received from a teacher before sustained japa, even though the praise-recitation form is openly available.

seoFaqs.question: Is reciting Siddha Kunjika really equivalent to chanting the full Devi Mahatmya?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional claim, repeated by Hindu Mirror, Effortless Mind Meditation and Yantrachants, is that one sincere recitation of the Siddha Kunjika Stotram is held to confer the merit of a full Saptashati parayana. Pandits explain this in two ways. First, since the stotram concentrates the Navarna Mantra, which is the central bija of the entire Saptashati, its repetition activates the same shakti in compressed form. Second, the hymn is described in its own concluding verses as 'guhyaat guhyataram' (most secret of secrets), with phala-shruti specifically equating its merit to that of full Saptashati recitation. In practice, most devotees do not treat it as a true substitute but as an adjunct: they chant the Saptashati when possible, and the Kunjika as a reliable daily Devi practice on other days.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Siddha Kunjika Stotram traditionally chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Effortless Mind Meditation, Yantrachants and Manblunder note that the stotram is most commonly chanted during Navratri (especially in homes that cannot complete a full daily Saptashati), on Tuesdays and Fridays year-round, and as part of an Ashtami or Navami sankalpa for any Devi festival. Many sadhakas recite it once at the start and once at the close of a Saptashati parayana, treating it as a kavacha around the longer text. Householders facing acute difficulty (illness, legal trouble, exam pressure) often undertake a 41-day sankalpa of one or three daily recitations. The traditional time is brahma muhurta (90 minutes before sunrise) or the early evening sandhya, with the chanter facing east or north before a ghee lamp.

seoFaqs.question: Are there any cautions traditionally attached to chanting the Siddha Kunjika?
seoFaqs.answer: Because the stotram contains the high-density Navarna and Kunjika bija mantras, traditional teachers including those summarised by Hindu Mirror and Manblunder recommend a few precautions. First, the chanter should bathe and wear clean clothes before recitation and should not chant casually after meat, alcohol or in an unsettled mind. Second, beginners are advised to receive at least basic guidance on pronunciation, since several of the bija syllables are easily mispronounced (kleem versus klim, vichche versus vichchhe). Third, the stotram should be completed once begun, with no breaks between its sections, and the closing phala-shruti verse should be included. These are not initiation requirements (the stotram is open to all householders), but ritual guidelines that protect the seriousness of a text traditionally classed as 'rahasya' (secret).
